content: We can borrow the deliberate reflective writing practice taught in high English language arts curricula (the language learning sense of reflexion) to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: requiring LLMs to structure their self-reflection as coherent, grammatically complete written arguments analogous to student reflective essays, rather than unstructured ad-hoc feedback, helps them catch more logical flaws in their outputs. Structured reflexion as a language learning exercise builds metacognitive awareness for human learners, and this same effect can be translated to structured metacognitive self-feedback for LLMs.
